textPlain: Good stuff.  I don't IOS much, but I've had a "days since <date>" shortcut on my phone homepage for some time.  Today shows 411 days.

I wish you luck. Looks like a well-written app.This is an area in which I have some experience (45 years clean). I've written a bunch of Recovery-assistive stuff, in my time, and continue to do so.In my experience, if you are a true addict (as opposed to someone that has developed bad habits, but doesn't have the obsessive/compulsive disorder), then no app will work. It's a fart against a tornado.But I feel as if everyone can benefit from self-discipline, and apps like this, work like fitness trackers. That can make major differences for many folks.However, there's no "silver bullet." Self-Discipline is hard work. A lot of people want "easy solutions," and they don't really exist (except in fiction).

Next year May I will be 20 years sober, quit drinking and smoking cold turkey.All I can remember was that smoking was the most difficult habit to quit because I was a chain smoker and the withdrawals are no joke. So yeah if you want to start on this sober journey, just be ready.This is a cool app, will download.

Clean idea and thoughtful execution. The no-ads, no-signup approach is refreshing—most sobriety apps feel bloated or pushy. Minimal streak tracking is exactly what many people want. Android version would be great to see.
I Am Sober is the most popular but it tries to upsell you so much. Weird when it does literally nothing except show your streak... glad to see some competition. I would use it but I'm on Android.
Question from somebody who is interested but who doesn't have a HN account: "How does it react if you break your streak?"
Just fyi - this app is from russia.And there are other free apps that do the same.
Where is the app to start e.g. drinking, it is morally relativistic to say these are 'bad habits'

That’s amazing — 20 years sober is truly inspiring! I quit smoking 281 days ago and drinking 240 days ago, and your story really motivates me to keep going. Thanks for sharing and for checking out the app!
Yeah but would be nice if he gets paid somehow
Thanks! I really appreciate it — and yes, I’m planning to release the Android version next year.

If you break your streak, the counter just starts again from day zero — no guilt trips, no judgment. The idea is to focus on progress, not perfection. You can reset your counter anytime from the counters edit screen.
Guilt by association is a pretty weak argument, you don't get to choose where you are born.

Thanks for the note! The app is made with care for people everywhere, regardless of where it’s built.
And yes — there are other apps out there, but I wanted to create something simple, ad-free, and focused purely on supporting your journey.

If you can track "anything else", I guess you can track your drinking streak, your days without exercising, how long you spent without calling your mom, or whatever "good habit" you want to quit.I don't think that's what most people are looking for though.
Someone watched to much Rick and Morty.Self destructing behaviour and hurting everyone you love are imo not a thing to be snarky about.
When someone is reaching for an app like this it's a bad habit.
